raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "Defining and prescribing the nature of proper (divine) service as emotional and attitudinal self-purification",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "There exists a form of 'service' (to the gods) whose essence is inner purity",
    "Violent passion, evil affection, rashness, vanity, and discontent are impurities that can and should be eliminated",
    "Discontent regarding gods or men is always to be avoided",
    "Such purity is attainable by a human being"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "Internally coherent; presents a consistent Stoic ethical ideal without logical contradiction",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "The Stoic worldview is accepted as true and practicing emotional detachment produces psychological benefit or eudaimonia",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "Passions or measured discontent are sometimes instrumentally valuable, or no divine 'service' of the described kind exists"
}
